intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer pre-digested weekly snapshots over day-by-day rows of raw data
facade.label: Weekly summary cards
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should default to weekly rolled-up views with drill-down to daily detail on demand
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should show daily granularity as primary view, requiring active filtering or scrolling to see trends

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar with experiment list improves navigation without requiring tabs or dropdowns
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure (sidebar vs tabs vs hybrid)
facade.label: Sidebar always visible
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick access to all experiments at once and prefers stable visual anchors over switching between views
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers full-width content space and doesn't need constant visibility of the experiment list

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer pre-digested weekly snapshots over day-by-day rows of raw data
evidence.implication: Dashboard should default to weekly rolled-up views with drill-down to daily detail on demand

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer gentle, approachable containers over stark rectangular boxes
facade.label: Soft rounded cards
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use curves and soft edges to feel less clinical, more supportive
facade.rejectImplies: Users want crisp, defined geometry — cards with clear boundaries and minimal border radius

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers organic and approachable visual language over clinical precision
facade.label: Soft, rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward warm, forgiving aesthetics—rounded cards, smooth transitions, hand-drawn elements. De-emphasize cold data tables.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clarity and structure over softness—crisp edges, grid-based layouts, stark contrast. Data visualization takes visual priority.

facade.hypothesis: Health dashboards work better when metric changes appear instantly rather than animating numbers up or down
facade.label: Instant numbers, no animation
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers snappy, immediate feedback over fluid motion—values speed and clarity over smoothness
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers seeing the change happen—values motion and visual confirmation over instant results

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a conversational, encouraging tone that feels like chatting with a friend rather than logging data into a clinical tool
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: The dashboard should use warm language, celebratory language, maybe casual emoji or illustrations; speak *to* the user like a coach or buddy tracking progress together
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer a more neutral, clinical, data-forward voice that gets out of the way; dashboard language should be minimal, scientific, and unadorned

facade.hypothesis: Users grasp 6-month patterns faster from a simple line chart than from a data table of 26 weeks of numbers
facade.axisTargeted: Visual encoding of time-series data (charts vs. tabular numbers)
facade.label: Trend lines over tables
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize sparklines and mini-charts on the dashboard; reserve tables for detailed export or deep-dive views
facade.rejectImplies: Users need the raw numbers visible by default; consider a hybrid card showing both small chart AND key metrics side-by-side

draft.title: Experiment Tracker Dashboard
draft.summary: A personal health dashboard for monitoring long-term experiments with visual progress tracking, experiment cards, and key metrics at a glance.
